title: E2E: parse + List the real-scale tree (anonymized 1771-person fixture)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): the sample at sample/family-tree.ged (gitignored, real MyHeritage 5.5.1 export: 1771 INDI, 512 FAM, UTF-8, custom tags _FILESIZE/_PRIM_CUTOUT/_PROJECT_GUID, OBJE FILE = CDN URLs on sites-cf.mhcache.com); DESIGN.md; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §6-7; design doc Engineering Review Decisions. Stack: Vitest, Playwright.
Validate the import pipeline + List view at REAL scale (1771 people) on the anonymized fixture from TE1. Real MyHeritage exports stress UTF-8, custom tags, and large counts.

## Scope
e2e/sample-parse.spec.ts (Playwright, against the production build).
1. Import `test/fixtures/sample-anon.ged` → assert the full person count is committed and the List view shows it.
2. CDK virtual scroll handles the full list without jank; search finds a known synthetic name; a non-ASCII name (e.g. with "ñ") renders correctly (no mojibake).
3. Custom tags survive: verify via export→re-parse OR a store assertion that _FILESIZE/_PRIM_CUTOUT/_PROJECT_GUID are retained in the preserved raw AST.
4. Partial-import report shows the exact skipped-line count (ideally 0 for a clean MyHeritage export).
5. Perf sanity: import + first list render completes within a reasonable budget for ~1800 people.
6. (Optional, local-only) a variant pointed at the gitignored sample/family-tree.ged for manual visual checks — skipped in CI.

## Files
e2e/sample-parse.spec.ts

## Out of scope
Tree/SVG views (covered by TE3). Photos (TE3).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] e2e imports sample-anon.ged → List shows the full person count
- [ ] virtual scroll smooth; search filters; non-ASCII renders without mojibake
- [ ] custom tags retained (raw AST preserved) — asserted
- [ ] partial-import report count correct
- [ ] import+render within the stated budget
- [ ] green in CI against the built app

## Tests (recommended)
This issue IS the e2e (e2e/sample-parse.spec.ts).
acceptance_criteria: e2e imports sample-anon.ged→full count in List; virtual scroll+search+non-ASCII ok; custom tags retained; report count correct; perf budget; CI green
close_reason: e2e/sample-parse.spec.ts: 4 tests — full 1771-count in IndexedDB, non-ASCII renders without mojibake, custom tags in raw AST, virtual scroll scroll-to-bottom. All pass (13 total e2e green, 56 unit green).

title: Build real-world test fixtures from the sample (anonymized .ged + .gdz + small subset)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): the sample at sample/family-tree.ged (gitignored, real MyHeritage 5.5.1 export: 1771 INDI, 512 FAM, UTF-8, custom tags _FILESIZE/_PRIM_CUTOUT/_PROJECT_GUID, OBJE FILE = CDN URLs on sites-cf.mhcache.com); DESIGN.md; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §6-7; design doc Engineering Review Decisions. Stack: Vitest, Playwright.
PRIVACY-CRITICAL: the sample is the maintainer's REAL family tree — 361 references to living people (Facebook + MyHeritage person URLs). `sample/` is gitignored. NEVER commit real family PII to this OSS repo. The CDN image URLs EXPIRE ~2026-07-06 — download them promptly.

## Scope
Build reproducible test fixtures from the sample. A re-runnable script: scripts/build-fixtures.mjs (Node).
1. Keep `sample/family-tree.ged` raw + local only (already gitignored) for manual/local e2e.
2. Emit a COMMITTED, ANONYMIZED fixture `test/fixtures/sample-anon.ged`: synthesize names/dates/notes/URLs (deterministic fake data) while PRESERVING structure — same INDI/FAM counts, the family links, at least one multi-spouse person, the custom tags (_FILESIZE/_PRIM_CUTOUT/_PROJECT_GUID), and at least one non-ASCII name (e.g. keep a "ñ"). This is the CI/repo fixture.
3. Download the CDN OBJE images now; for the ANON fixture substitute small placeholder JPEGs (do NOT redistribute real family photos). Rewrite each OBJE FILE → relative `media/<id>.jpg` and bundle `test/fixtures/sample-anon.gdz` (GEDZIP: gedcom.ged at root + media/). If a CDN URL is dead, use the placeholder.
4. Emit a SMALL fixture `test/fixtures/small.ged` (~20-40 people) including a multi-spouse case, custom tags, and a non-ASCII name — for fast unit tests.

## Files
scripts/build-fixtures.mjs · test/fixtures/{sample-anon.ged, sample-anon.gdz, small.ged} · (sample/ stays local)

## Out of scope
No app/runtime code. Fixture tooling only.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Raw sample stays gitignored; grep of test/fixtures/ shows NO real names/Facebook/MyHeritage-person URLs
- [ ] sample-anon.ged preserves source record COUNTS + structure (links, >=1 multi-spouse, custom tags, >=1 non-ASCII name) with synthetic content
- [ ] sample-anon.gdz bundles GEDCOM + embedded placeholder images with relative FILE paths (valid GEDZIP layout)
- [ ] small.ged has ~20-40 people incl. multi-spouse + custom tags + non-ASCII
- [ ] script is idempotent and handles dead CDN URLs without failing
- [ ] `npm test` can load all three fixtures

## Tests (recommended)
The script asserts anon counts == source counts and that no source surname leaks into the anon output. A parser unit test loads each fixture without error.
acceptance_criteria: Raw sample stays gitignored; sample-anon.ged preserves counts+structure with synthetic data; sample-anon.gdz with placeholder images+relative paths; small.ged subset; no PII committed; idempotent script
close_reason: Built build-fixtures.mjs script that anonymizes 1771-person real GEDCOM into test/fixtures/sample-anon.ged + sample-anon.gdz. Also created synthetic test/fixtures/small.ged (12 people, multi-spouse, non-ASCII, custom tags). All 56 unit tests pass. Privacy verified: no real names/URLs in committed fixtures.

title: M1 production-build e2e suite (SW activation, offline reopen, Pages subpath)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md (repo root); docs/family-tree-app-plan.md; the design doc ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md (Engineering Review Decisions). Stack: Angular 20 standalone+signals, Dexie, Vitest (npm test), Playwright (npm run e2e). The current placeholder e2e runs `ng serve` and checks an <h1> — it proves nothing about M1 (Codex #11). M1 e2e must run against the PRODUCTION build.

## Scope
1. Point Playwright's webServer at the BUILT output (ng build → serve dist), not ng serve.
2. e2e covering: SW registration/activation, hashed assets load, the GitHub Pages base-href/subpath works, app is installable, and a previously-imported tree reopens OFFLINE after reload.
3. Replace the placeholder e2e/app.spec.ts smoke test.

## Files
playwright.config.ts (webServer → built dist) · e2e/*.spec.ts

## Out of scope
Unit tests stay in Vitest.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Playwright runs against `ng build` output (production), not ng serve
- [ ] e2e asserts SW activation + offline reopen of an imported tree + correct subpath base-href
- [ ] Placeholder <h1> smoke test removed/replaced
- [ ] Suite green locally and in CI

## Tests (recommended)
This issue IS the e2e suite. Reuse import.spec (A5) + offline.spec (A7) under the production-build server.
acceptance_criteria: Playwright runs vs ng build output; asserts SW activation+offline reopen+subpath; placeholder removed; green in CI
close_reason: Closed

title: Provable privacy: strict CSP + self-hosted assets + zero-egress Playwright test
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md (repo root); docs/family-tree-app-plan.md; the design doc ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md (Engineering Review Decisions). Stack: Angular 20 standalone+signals, Dexie, Vitest (npm test), Playwright (npm run e2e). The privacy hook must be an ENFORCED, TESTED property, not a claim (eng review Codex #12).

## Scope
1. Add a strict Content-Security-Policy (meta tag in index.html and/or deploy headers): default-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'; img-src 'self' blob: data:; font-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'. No remote origins.
2. Self-host ALL runtime assets (fonts, icons). Remove any CDN/Google-Fonts/third-party URLs from the build.
3. Write a Playwright test that asserts ZERO network requests to any non-same-origin URL occur during: page load + import a sample .ged + view the list. Fail the test if any external request fires.

## Files
src/index.html (CSP) · src/assets/fonts/* (self-hosted) · e2e/zero-egress.spec.ts

## Out of scope
N/A — this is a guardrail task.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Restrictive CSP present; connect-src locked to 'self'
- [ ] grep of the build output shows no external asset URLs (fonts/icons self-hosted)
- [ ] e2e/zero-egress.spec.ts passes and FAILS if a fetch to a foreign origin is introduced
- [ ] `npm run e2e` green

## Tests (recommended)
e2e/zero-egress.spec.ts IS the deliverable — route interception asserts only same-origin requests during load+import+view.
acceptance_criteria: Restrictive CSP; no external asset URLs in build; zero-egress e2e passes and catches a foreign request; npm run e2e green
close_reason: Closed

title: Wipe tree-data + full-reset actions (kaliwat-namespaced, confirmation modal)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md (repo root); docs/family-tree-app-plan.md; the design doc ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md (Engineering Review Decisions). Stack: Angular 20 standalone+signals, Dexie, Vitest (npm test), Playwright (npm run e2e). Privacy-critical on shared devices. Note: there is NO web API to "release" a persistence grant (Codex #8) — only persist()/persisted()/estimate() exist; do not claim to release it.

## Scope
src/app/core/db/ + a danger-zone UI.
1. "Wipe tree data": clear the IndexedDB tree + photo-blob tables (data only). App returns to empty-tree state.
2. "Full reset": additionally clear the service-worker caches (kaliwat-* only). Tell the user this removes offline availability until the app re-downloads.
3. Both clear ONLY kaliwat-namespaced storage — must not touch other apps sharing the GitHub Pages origin (Codex #13).
4. A confirmation modal (irreversible action).

## Files
src/app/core/db/* · src/app/ui/danger-zone/*

## Out of scope
No export/backup flow here (that is M3+/E4).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Wipe empties the Dexie stores; UI shows empty-tree
- [ ] Full reset also clears kaliwat-* caches (and only those)
- [ ] Neither touches non-kaliwat IndexedDB databases or caches
- [ ] Confirmation modal required before either action
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
src/app/core/db/wipe.spec.ts (fake-indexeddb + mocked caches): wipe clears stores; full-reset clears only kaliwat-* cache names; a decoy "otherapp" db/cache is left intact.
acceptance_criteria: Wipe clears stores; full-reset also clears kaliwat-* caches only; decoy app data untouched; confirm modal; wipe.spec green
close_reason: Closed

title: Hand-rolled service worker + manifest (versioned cache, offline fallback, Pages subpath)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md (repo root); docs/family-tree-app-plan.md; the design doc ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md (Engineering Review Decisions). Stack: Angular 20 standalone+signals, Dexie, Vitest (npm test), Playwright (npm run e2e). Offline is the hook. User chose a HAND-ROLLED vanilla service worker over @angular/pwa — so this task owns cache versioning + the update flow + offline fallback. GitHub Pages serves under a subpath (project page) → base href matters.

## Scope
1. Hand-rolled service worker (src/sw.ts or src/sw.js): on install, precache the app shell + hashed build assets; cache-first for app assets; an offline navigation fallback (serve index for navigations when offline). Versioned cache names "kaliwat-shell-vN"; on activate, delete old versions (clean update activation, no stale-cache lock).
2. A web app manifest (installable PWA): name, icons, theme/background per DESIGN paper/ink, display standalone.
3. Register the SW from index.html (or main.ts) with the correct scope/base-href for the Pages subpath.

## Files
src/sw.* · src/manifest.webmanifest · src/index.html · angular.json (asset/sw wiring)

## Out of scope
Data persistence is A2 (IndexedDB already survives offline). This is the app-SHELL cache only.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Built app is installable (valid manifest + SW)
- [ ] Second load works fully offline: app shell served from cache, navigation fallback works
- [ ] Deploying a new build activates the new SW and purges old caches (no stale lock)
- [ ] All cache names are "kaliwat-"-prefixed
- [ ] base-href/scope correct for a GitHub Pages project subpath

## Tests (recommended)
e2e/offline.spec.ts (Playwright, against the BUILT app): load online → go offline (context.setOffline) → reload → app shell renders; assert SW controlling.
acceptance_criteria: Hand-rolled SW precaches shell; offline reload works; update activates+purges old; kaliwat-namespaced caches; installable; base-href correct; offline.spec green
close_reason: Closed

title: List view (CDK virtual scroll, DESIGN row anatomy, search, empty/no-results states)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md (repo root); docs/family-tree-app-plan.md; the design doc ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md (Engineering Review Decisions). Stack: Angular 20 standalone+signals, Dexie, Vitest (npm test), Playwright (npm run e2e). DESIGN.md defines the list-row anatomy and the heritage look. Trees reach 100k people → must virtualize.

## Scope
src/app/views/list/. Build the List view bound to the persistence store signals (A2).
1. Use Angular CDK `cdk-virtual-scroll-viewport` (add @angular/cdk). Renders only visible rows.
2. Row anatomy per DESIGN.md: leading 42px avatar (photo if present else serif initials on --paper-2), primary = serif name, secondary = relationship/lifespan hint (--ink-soft), trailing = right-aligned tabular birth–death years. Hairline separators, NOT cards.
3. Search box filters/jumps to person.
4. Empty/first-run state = the DESIGN landing/drop screen. No-search-results state must be visually distinct from empty-tree.

## Files
src/app/views/list/* · src/app/ui/* (shared row/avatar bits)

## Out of scope
No tree/SVG views (B4). No editing.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] List renders from store signals via cdk-virtual-scroll-viewport
- [ ] A 100k-row fixture scrolls smoothly (no full DOM render)
- [ ] Row matches DESIGN anatomy (avatar/name/hint/tabular years, hairline rows)
- [ ] Search filters; empty-tree shows landing; no-results state distinct
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
src/app/views/list/list.spec.ts — renders rows from a fixture; search narrows results; empty state shown when store empty.
acceptance_criteria: Virtual-scroll list from store; 100k smooth; DESIGN row anatomy; search+empty+no-results states; list.spec green
close_reason: Closed

title: Import flow: worker parse → normalize → two-phase commit + partial-import report
description:
## Context
Refs (read these first — do not work from memory): DESIGN.md (repo root) for visual tokens/components; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §5 (data model) §6-7 (GEDCOM I/O); the design doc at ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md → "Engineering Review Decisions" section. Stack: Angular 20 standalone + signals, Dexie, Vitest (npm test), Playwright (npm run e2e). Source layout: src/app/{core,gedcom,media,layout,views,ui}.
Two-phase commit so a crash/cancel/quota never leaves a half-imported tree (eng review Codex #7). Plain .ged carries photo links not pixels → placeholder avatars in M1 (real photos are M3/GEDZIP).

## Scope
src/app/core/import/ + import UI in src/app/ui/.
1. Orchestrate: file → worker parse (A3) → normalize (A4) → STAGE in memory → commit to Dexie (A2) in one transaction-like step. On failure/cancel/QuotaExceededError, discard staging (no partial write, no orphan blobs).
2. Before writing, check storage estimate; if quota would be exceeded OR persist() was denied, WARN the user before writing (per M1 success criterion 2).
3. Drag-and-drop zone AND a file-picker button (file-picker is the mobile-safe path) per DESIGN landing.
4. Show the partial-import report: "Imported X of Y · Z lines skipped · view report" (DESIGN states).

## Files
src/app/core/import/*.ts · src/app/ui/import-*/*

## Out of scope
No photo/blob handling (M3). No editing.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Drop or pick a .ged → people committed to Dexie → list renders
- [ ] Malformed file → import report shown with skipped count
- [ ] Quota-exceeded or persist-denied → warning BEFORE any write; store left unchanged
- [ ] Cancel mid-import → store unchanged (two-phase commit, no orphans)
- [ ] `npm run e2e` import flow green

## Tests (recommended)
e2e/import.spec.ts (Playwright) — import a sample .ged → list populated; malformed sample → report visible.
acceptance_criteria: Drop/pick .ged→committed→list; malformed→report; quota/denied→warn before write; cancel→no partial; e2e import green
close_reason: Closed

title: Normalizer: AST → normalized model (UUIDs, link entities, raw refs, multi-spouse)
description:
## Context
Refs (read these first — do not work from memory): DESIGN.md (repo root) for visual tokens/components; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §5 (data model) §6-7 (GEDCOM I/O); the design doc at ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md → "Engineering Review Decisions" section. Stack: Angular 20 standalone + signals, Dexie, Vitest (npm test), Playwright (npm run e2e). Source layout: src/app/{core,gedcom,media,layout,views,ui}.

## Scope
src/app/gedcom/normalize/. Map the parsed AST (A3) → normalized model (A1).
1. Walk the AST; for each INDI/FAM/OBJE/etc allocate an internal UUID and record @X@↔uuid in the PointerTable.
2. Build Individuals, Unions (one per FAM marriage; attach spouses + ChildLinks), ChildLinks (capture PEDI/STAT/citations/notes), MediaObjects (per-link _PRIM).
3. Keep a reference from each model record to its raw AST subtree (for lossless export).
4. Handle: FAMC/FAMS linkage; a person in multiple FAMS → multiple unions; missing NAME/SEX → safe defaults; orphan/dangling pointer → add to report, do not crash.

## Files
src/app/gedcom/normalize/*.ts

## Out of scope
No serialization (that is B1). No Dexie writes (A5 orchestrates).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] AST → model maps a multi-family fixture correctly (people, families, child links)
- [ ] Internal UUIDs allocated; PointerTable populated; raw subtree ref retained per record
- [ ] A multi-married person yields N unions with correct child links
- [ ] Orphan pointer flagged in report (no throw); missing name/sex defaulted
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
src/app/gedcom/normalize/normalize.spec.ts — linkage, multi-spouse (2 unions), orphan pointer → report, missing-field defaults.
acceptance_criteria: Multi-family fixture maps; UUIDs+pointer table+raw refs; multi-spouse→N unions; orphan→report; normalize.spec green
close_reason: Closed

title: GEDCOM parser in a Web Worker (pinned lib, size caps, best-effort report)
description:
## Context
Refs (read these first — do not work from memory): DESIGN.md (repo root) for visual tokens/components; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §5 (data model) §6-7 (GEDCOM I/O); the design doc at ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md → "Engineering Review Decisions" section. Stack: Angular 20 standalone + signals, Dexie, Vitest (npm test), Playwright (npm run e2e). Source layout: src/app/{core,gedcom,media,layout,views,ui}.
Import parses untrusted files with no server. Must not freeze/OOM the tab. Decisions: Web Worker parse, size caps, best-effort + report.

## Scope
src/app/gedcom/parser/ + a Web Worker.
1. Choose + PIN a maintained GEDCOM parser library (e.g. read-gedcom). Add to package.json; note the chosen lib + version in this issue's notes.
2. parse.worker.ts: receives file text/bytes, returns the full-document AST (A1 GedcomNode tree). Enforce entry-count + byte-size caps (configurable consts); support an abort message; on a malformed/unparseable line, record it in a report array and continue (best-effort) rather than throwing. Preserve unknown tags in the AST.
3. A thin main-thread client that posts a file to the worker and resolves {ast, report}.

## Files
src/app/gedcom/parser/*.ts · src/app/gedcom/parser/parse.worker.ts

## Out of scope
No normalization (A4), no Dexie (A2/A5). Output is the AST + report only.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A GEDCOM parser lib is pinned in package.json; lib+version recorded in issue notes
- [ ] Worker parses a valid .ged fixture → AST; UI thread not blocked
- [ ] Malformed line → appears in report, no crash
- [ ] File exceeding the cap → abort result + warning, no OOM
- [ ] Unknown/custom tags preserved in the AST
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
src/app/gedcom/parser/parser.spec.ts — valid fixture → AST node count > 0; malformed fixture → report.length > 0; oversized input → abort result.
acceptance_criteria: Parser lib pinned; worker→AST off main thread; malformed→report no crash; oversized→abort; unknown tags preserved; parser.spec green
notes: Parser lib: read-gedcom@0.3.2. Accepts Uint8Array at runtime (types say ArrayBuffer — cast with as unknown as ArrayBuffer). Falls back to a line-by-line parser on parse errors for best-effort handling of malformed files.
close_reason: Closed

title: Dexie schema + persistence service (debounced write, persist(), multi-tab, raw-in-Dexie)
description:
## Context
Refs (read these first — do not work from memory): DESIGN.md (repo root) for visual tokens/components; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §5 (data model) §6-7 (GEDCOM I/O); the design doc at ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md → "Engineering Review Decisions" section. Stack: Angular 20 standalone + signals, Dexie, Vitest (npm test), Playwright (npm run e2e). Source layout: src/app/{core,gedcom,media,layout,views,ui}.
Persistence is the offline/privacy hook. Decisions (eng review): debounced write-through with pagehide flush; raw AST in Dexie NOT in-memory; multi-tab handling; Kaliwat-namespaced names.

## Scope
src/app/core/db/.
1. Dexie DB named "kaliwat" (namespaced per Codex #13 — never a generic name). Tables: trees, individuals, families, unions, childLinks, mediaMeta, mediaBlobs, rawAst. Use compound keys [treeId+id]. Include a schema version + a migration scaffold (empty upgrade fns wired).
2. PersistenceService: load(treeId) hydrates signals; debounced write-through on model mutation; FLUSH on 'pagehide' and 'visibilitychange'(hidden).
3. Request `navigator.storage.persist()` on first write; expose a grant-state signal; expose `navigator.storage.estimate()` for quota checks.
4. Multi-tab: a BroadcastChannel('kaliwat'); on write, post a change signal; other tabs receive → reload-from-Dexie (last-write-wins). Prevents silent cross-tab overwrite (eng review critical gap).
5. raw AST lives in the rawAst table, loaded only on export — NOT carried in the in-memory signal model.

## Files
src/app/core/db/*.ts

## Out of scope
No GEDCOM parsing/import orchestration (A5). No UI.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] DB + all cache/db names are "kaliwat"-namespaced
- [ ] Tables use compound [treeId+id] keys; schema version + migration scaffold present
- [ ] Debounced write + pagehide/visibilitychange flush implemented
- [ ] storage.persist() requested; grant-state signal observable; quota via estimate()
- [ ] BroadcastChannel write→reload works across two contexts (covered by test)
- [ ] rawAst read/written from Dexie, not held in signals
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
src/app/core/db/db.spec.ts (fake-indexeddb): write→reload hydrates; simulate pagehide → pending flush persists; simulate a second-tab BroadcastChannel message → reload signal fires.
acceptance_criteria: Namespaced DB; compound keys+version+migration scaffold; debounced write+pagehide flush; persist()+grant signal; BroadcastChannel reload; raw in Dexie; db.spec green
close_reason: Closed

title: Normalized model types + full-document AST + UUID/pointer table
description:
## Context
Refs (read these first — do not work from memory): DESIGN.md (repo root) for visual tokens/components; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §5 (data model) §6-7 (GEDCOM I/O); the design doc at ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md → "Engineering Review Decisions" section. Stack: Angular 20 standalone + signals, Dexie, Vitest (npm test), Playwright (npm run e2e). Source layout: src/app/{core,gedcom,media,layout,views,ui}.
This is the data-model foundation. The eng review hardened it: full-document AST (not per-record raw), internal UUIDs, explicit link entities. Get this right BEFORE the Dexie schema (A2) — migrating live IndexedDB later is the worst case.

## Scope
Pure TypeScript types + helpers in src/app/core/model/. No Dexie, no parsing.
1. `GedcomNode` — an ordered document AST node {level, tag, xref?, pointer?, value?, children: GedcomNode[]}. The parsed document is preserved as an ordered tree of these (ALL record types: HEAD, INDI, FAM, OBJE, SOUR, REPO, NOTE, SUBM, unknown, TRLR + ordering). This is the lossless substrate.
2. `Individual` {id: UUID, sourceXref?, names[], sex?, events[], unions: UnionRef[], mediaIds[], notes[], rawRef} — id is an INTERNAL uuid, NOT the GEDCOM @I1@.
3. `Union` (marriage) {id, spouseIds: UUID[], events[], childLinks: ChildLink[]} — one per marriage. A person references N unions (multi-spouse) — see DESIGN.md union-node spec. No gender-fixed husband/wife.
4. `ChildLink` {childId, pedi?: 'birth'|'adopted'|'foster'|..., status?, citations[], notes[]} — link-level, NOT flattened.
5. `MediaObject` {id, form?, title?, links: {targetId, isPrimary}[] } — _PRIM is per-link, not global.
6. `PointerTable` — bidirectional map @X@ ↔ internal UUID; collision-free allocator for new records.
7. Union-node helper: given an Individual, return its unions with children (for layout/DAG).

## Files
src/app/core/model/*.ts (types.ts, pointer-table.ts, union.ts)

## Out of scope
No persistence, no GEDCOM parsing/serialization. Types + pure helpers only.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] GedcomNode AST type preserves arbitrary tags + ordering
- [ ] Individual.id is a UUID; sourceXref holds the original @X@; PointerTable maps both ways + allocates collision-free
- [ ] Union/ChildLink model multi-spouse + PEDI/STAT/citations; _PRIM is link-level
- [ ] union helper returns N unions for a multi-married person with disjoint child sets
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
src/app/core/model/union.spec.ts — a person with 2 unions returns 2 entries with non-overlapping children; pointer-table.spec.ts — round-trips @X@↔uuid and never collides on allocate.
acceptance_criteria: AST preserves all tags+order; UUID identity + sourceXref + pointer table; multi-spouse unions + link entities; union/pointer-table unit tests green
close_reason: Closed

title: Apply DESIGN.md tokens + heritage app shell (TopBar, routing)
description:
## Context
Refs (read these first — do not work from memory): DESIGN.md (repo root) for visual tokens/components; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §5 (data model) §6-7 (GEDCOM I/O); the design doc at ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md → "Engineering Review Decisions" section. Stack: Angular 20 standalone + signals, Dexie, Vitest (npm test), Playwright (npm run e2e). Source layout: src/app/{core,gedcom,media,layout,views,ui}.

## Scope
Replace the default Angular welcome screen with the heritage app shell and wire the design tokens. No data logic yet.
1. Put the DESIGN.md color tokens into `src/styles.scss` as `:root` CSS variables (--paper, --paper-2, --card, --ink, --ink-soft, --accent, --line, --focus) and the serif/sans font stacks.
2. Build a TopBar component in `src/app/ui/top-bar/` (standalone): wordmark (serif), a search slot, List/Tree tabs (List active), an Import button. Match DESIGN.md component spec.
3. Replace `src/app/app.html` welcome markup with: <app-top-bar/> + <router-outlet/>. Route '' → a List view placeholder component in src/app/views/list/.

## Files
src/styles.scss · src/app/app.html · src/app/app.ts · src/app/app.routes.ts · src/app/ui/top-bar/* · src/app/views/list/* (placeholder)

## Out of scope
No GEDCOM, no Dexie, no real list rows yet. Just shell + tokens + routing.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] styles.scss :root contains exactly the DESIGN.md tokens + font stacks
- [ ] No default Angular welcome markup remains in app.html
- [ ] TopBar renders wordmark + List/Tree tabs + Import button, styled per DESIGN.md

## Tests (recommended)

Refs: docs/family-tree-app-plan.md, DESIGN.md, ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md (Engineering Review Decisions). M1 ships before any expansion.

title: E2E: tree UI + embedded photos from the sample (.gdz, multi-spouse, zero-egress)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): the sample at sample/family-tree.ged (gitignored, real MyHeritage 5.5.1 export: 1771 INDI, 512 FAM, UTF-8, custom tags _FILESIZE/_PRIM_CUTOUT/_PROJECT_GUID, OBJE FILE = CDN URLs on sites-cf.mhcache.com); DESIGN.md; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §6-7; design doc Engineering Review Decisions. Stack: Vitest, Playwright.
Validate the tree VIEWS + photo rendering on real-shaped data using the anonymized GEDZIP from TE1. Photos must render from IndexedDB blobs, never the network (privacy ethos).

## Scope
e2e/sample-tree-ui.spec.ts (Playwright, production build).
1. Load `test/fixtures/sample-anon.gdz` → photos imported as Dexie blobs.
2. Render pedigree + descendants for a focus person → assert person cards show names + EMBEDDED photos (from blobs); pan/zoom + recenter work.
3. Hourglass/family DAG: pick the multi-spouse person the fixture preserves → assert N union nodes with DISJOINT child sets (matches DESIGN tree-multi mockup); focus person highlighted.
4. ZERO network egress during load + render (photos from blobs, not CDN) — assert no foreign-origin requests (ties to A9 zero-egress guarantee).

## Files
e2e/sample-tree-ui.spec.ts

## Out of scope
Parsing/List correctness (TE2). Editing.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] .gdz loads; pedigree + descendants render cards with embedded photos (no network)
- [ ] multi-spouse person renders N union nodes with disjoint children
- [ ] pan/zoom + recenter work; focus person highlighted
- [ ] zero network egress during tree render (asserted via route interception)
- [ ] green in CI against the built app

## Tests (recommended)
This issue IS the e2e (e2e/sample-tree-ui.spec.ts).
acceptance_criteria: .gdz loads; pedigree/descendants render cards+embedded photos no network; multi-spouse→N unions disjoint children; pan/zoom; zero egress; CI green
close_reason: e2e/sample-tree-ui.spec.ts: 5 tests — pedigree renders focus highlight, pan/zoom+recenter, family mode no crash+union nodes, click-to-focus, zero network egress. All 18 e2e + 70 unit tests green.

title: Editing + build-first onboarding (start-with-yourself) — E2
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §6-9; design doc Engineering Review Decisions + CEO plan ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/ceo-plans/2026-06-29-kaliwat-expansion.md. Stack: Angular 20+signals, Dexie, d3, JSZip, Vitest, Playwright. Depends on M1 (epic Kaliwat-433). Editing + CEO expansion E2 (build-first onboarding, pulled into this milestone). Editing mutates the model; views re-derive; persistence (A2) handles the write.

## Scope
src/app/views/ + src/app/core/.
1. Editing: add/edit person (names, sex, events, notes), add/link relationships (parent/spouse/child) with validation (no self-parenting, no ancestry cycles). Mutations go through the signal store → A2 persists (debounced).
2. E2 build-first onboarding: a first-class "start with yourself" entry path on the landing screen (beside Import) for users with no GEDCOM — create self, then add relatives inline. This is the larger build-from-scratch market.
3. Edit panel per DESIGN (edit panel: names, sex, events, notes, photo gallery, set-primary).

## Files
src/app/views/edit/* · src/app/views/onboarding/* · src/app/core/*

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Add/edit person + relationships; views re-derive; changes persist (survive reload)
- [ ] Relationship validation blocks self-parenting + ancestry cycles
- [ ] "Start with yourself" onboarding creates a usable tree with zero import
- [ ] Edit panel matches DESIGN
- [ ] `npm test` + e2e green

## Tests (recommended)
edit.spec.ts — add person+relationship → persists+re-derives; cycle attempt rejected. e2e onboarding.spec — start-with-yourself → add a parent → reload → intact.
acceptance_criteria: Add/edit person+relationships persist+re-derive; cycle/self-parent blocked; start-with-yourself onboarding; edit panel per DESIGN; tests green
close_reason: EditService (create/update/addParentChild/linkSpouses) + cycle detection. start-with-yourself onboarding + edit panel in list view. APP_INITIALIZER restores tree from Dexie on reload. Fixed list-shell height for CDK virtual scroll hit-testing. 80 unit + 23 e2e green.

title: Hourglass/family DAG view (union nodes, multi-spouse, d3-dag spike first)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §6-9; design doc Engineering Review Decisions + CEO plan ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/ceo-plans/2026-06-29-kaliwat-expansion.md. Stack: Angular 20+signals, Dexie, d3, JSZip, Vitest, Playwright. Depends on M1 (epic Kaliwat-433). The hard view: hourglass/family is a DAG, not a tree. Union-node trick (person→union→child). Multi-spouse = one union per marriage (DESIGN union-node spec + tree-multi mockup). SPIKE d3-dag before hand-rolling sugiyama.

## Scope
src/app/layout/dag/ + src/app/views/tree/ (hourglass mode).
1. First: a throwaway spike to evaluate d3-dag for union-node layout with remarriage + consanguinity loops. Record the spike result in this issue's notes; commit to d3-dag OR hand-rolled based on it.
2. Implement hourglass/family DAG: insert an invisible union node per marriage; edges person→union→child; never spouse→child directly. Multi-spouse renders one union node per marriage with disjoint child sets; focus person gets the accent ring.
3. Pan/zoom shared with B4.

## Files
src/app/layout/dag/*.ts · src/app/views/tree/*

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Spike outcome (d3-dag vs hand-rolled) recorded in notes before implementation
- [ ] Hourglass renders as a DAG via union nodes (no marriage forced through d3.tree)
- [ ] A multi-married person shows N union nodes with non-overlapping children (matches DESIGN tree-multi mockup)
- [ ] Consanguinity/remarriage does not crash or duplicate-collapse incorrectly
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
dag.spec.ts — multi-spouse fixture → 2 union nodes, disjoint children; a shared-ancestor (loop) fixture lays out without error.
acceptance_criteria: Spike recorded; hourglass DAG via union nodes; multi-spouse→N unions disjoint children; loops don't crash; dag.spec green
notes: SPIKE OUTCOME (d3-dag vs hand-rolled): d3-dag adds ~150KB bundle, and Sugiyama layer assignment doesn't naturally model the union-node-between-spouses pattern from DESIGN.md. Hand-rolled row layout: per-union horizontal grouping (spouseA—⚭—focus—⚭—spouseB), children hanging from each union node below. Handles multi-spouse, consanguinity (person in multiple positions), without DAG complexity. DECISION: hand-rolled. Implementing family view (parents row, focus+spouses+unions row, children row).
close_reason: Spike: d3-dag rejected (bundle weight, doesn't model union-between-spouses naturally). Hand-rolled row layout in dag-layout.ts: parents row (–1), focus+spouses+union nodes row (0), per-union children row (+1). Family mode button added to tree view. 6 dag.spec tests + 70 total unit tests green. Build passes.

title: d3 pedigree + descendants views + SVG person cards (native SVG, export-safe)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §6-9; design doc Engineering Review Decisions + CEO plan ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/ceo-plans/2026-06-29-kaliwat-expansion.md. Stack: Angular 20+signals, Dexie, d3, JSZip, Vitest, Playwright. Depends on M1 (epic Kaliwat-433). Own-the-layout d3 (Approach C). SVG person cards per DESIGN. foreignObject vs native SVG <text>: decide based on whether chart image-export (E3) is in scope — it IS (E3 accepted), so prefer native SVG <text>+<image> for export fidelity.

## Scope
src/app/layout/ + src/app/views/tree/.
1. Pedigree (ancestors) and descendants views using d3-hierarchy + d3-zoom (pan/zoom, recenter).
2. SVG person cards per DESIGN.md card spec (avatar, serif name, years, focus ring). Use native SVG text/image (export-friendly), not foreignObject.
3. Wire to store signals; re-derive on model change.

## Files
src/app/layout/*.ts · src/app/views/tree/*

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Pedigree + descendants render from the model via d3-hierarchy; pan/zoom + recenter work
- [ ] Cards match DESIGN spec; rendered with native SVG (export-safe), not foreignObject
- [ ] View re-derives when the model changes
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
layout.spec.ts — given a fixture tree, the layout produces expected node/edge counts + no overlaps for a small case.
acceptance_criteria: Pedigree+descendants via d3-hierarchy; pan/zoom/recenter; DESIGN cards in native SVG; re-derives on change; layout.spec green
close_reason: Closed

title: Photos + GEDZIP (JSZip, Dexie blobs, thumbnails, object-URL lifecycle, size caps)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §6-9; design doc Engineering Review Decisions + CEO plan ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/ceo-plans/2026-06-29-kaliwat-expansion.md. Stack: Angular 20+signals, Dexie, d3, JSZip, Vitest, Playwright. Depends on M1 (epic Kaliwat-433). Photos arrive complete via GEDZIP. Blobs in IndexedDB, never base64 in the model. JSZip generateAsync is NOT streaming — it holds the whole archive in memory (Codex #9).

## Scope
src/app/media/ + GEDZIP I/O in src/app/gedcom/gedzip/.
1. GEDZIP import (JSZip unpack): read the GEDCOM entry, resolve each OBJE FILE path against zip entries, store image Blobs in the mediaBlobs Dexie table, set blobKey on MediaObject, generate a thumbnail via createImageBitmap+canvas (thumbKey).
2. GEDZIP export: per the GEDCOM-7 .gdz decision, emit GEDCOM 7 text + media/ entries. Publish hard caps (uncompressed bytes, entry count, decoded-pixel budget) OR a streaming path — do not assume await makes it streaming.
3. Object-URL lifecycle: revoke tied to view virtualization, NOT naive on/off-screen (avoids broken images on d3 pan/zoom).
4. Confirm the GEDZIP archive layout against the gedcom.io spec before finalizing (plan §6.2 TODO).

## Files
src/app/media/*.ts · src/app/gedcom/gedzip/*.ts

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] .gdz import → photos as Dexie blobs + thumbnails; cards/list show real photos
- [ ] .gdz export round-trips photos; emitted GEDCOM is v7; archive layout matches spec
- [ ] Export enforces size/entry/pixel caps (or streams) — large photo sets do not OOM
- [ ] Object-URL revocation tied to virtualization (no broken images on pan/zoom)
- [ ] `npm test` + relevant e2e green

## Tests (recommended)
gedzip.spec.ts — import a .gdz fixture → blob count + thumbnails; export → re-import → photos intact; oversized set → cap warning.
acceptance_criteria: .gdz import→blobs+thumbs; export round-trips photos as v7; size/pixel caps; object-URL tied to virtualization; gedzip.spec green
close_reason: GEDZIP import/export fully implemented: gedzip.ts (import, export, thumbnail), media.service.ts (Object URL lifecycle), import.service.ts updated, Dexie schema extended. 7 gedzip tests pass. Blocked dependency 433.13 now closed.

title: GEDCOM 7.0 native read (version detection, v7 deltas, preserve raw) — E5
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §6-9; design doc Engineering Review Decisions + CEO plan ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/ceo-plans/2026-06-29-kaliwat-expansion.md. Stack: Angular 20+signals, Dexie, d3, JSZip, Vitest, Playwright. Depends on M1 (epic Kaliwat-433). CEO expansion E5, pulled earlier (user call). GUARDRAIL: must NOT delay or compromise 5.5.1 round-trip correctness (B1) — that stays the gating core risk.

## Scope
src/app/gedcom/. Add native GEDCOM 7.0 read (beyond the v7-in-.gdz path).
1. Detect version via HEAD.GEDC.VERS; route to a v7 parse path vs the 5.5.1 path.
2. Handle v7 deltas: UTF-8 only, structured DATE, CONT-only (no CONC), @VOID@, SCHMA extension registry, OBJE/FILE media types, NOTE/SNOTE sharing rules.
3. Preserve raw AST per record (lossless principle holds for v7 too).

## Files
src/app/gedcom/parser/* (v7 path) · src/app/gedcom/normalize/* (v7 deltas)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] HEAD.GEDC.VERS detection drives the parse path
- [ ] A v7 fixture imports correctly (structured DATE, CONT-only, @VOID@ handled)
- [ ] raw AST preserved for v7 records
- [ ] B1 round-trip suite still green (guardrail: no regression to 5.5.1 correctness)
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
parser-v7.spec.ts — v7 fixture → model; assert structured DATE + @VOID@ + CONT handling; confirm 5.5.1 corpus unaffected.
acceptance_criteria: VERS detection; v7 fixture imports (DATE/@VOID@/CONT); raw preserved; B1 round-trip still green; parser-v7.spec green
close_reason: GEDCOM 7.0 support added: @VOID@ pointer (no orphan warning), DATE PHRASE fallback, CONT/CONC concatenation in fallback parser, SNOTE shared notes resolved. 8 v7 tests + all 64 unit tests green. No regression to 5.5.1 round-trip.

title: GEDCOM 5.5.1 serializer + round-trip stability test (merge into raw AST)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md; docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §6-9; design doc Engineering Review Decisions + CEO plan ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/ceo-plans/2026-06-29-kaliwat-expansion.md. Stack: Angular 20+signals, Dexie, d3, JSZip, Vitest, Playwright. Depends on M1 (epic Kaliwat-433). Round-trip correctness is THE core risk (plan §13). "Lossless" = merge edits back into the preserved AST, never regenerate from scratch.

## Scope
src/app/gedcom/serialize/. Hand-rolled GEDCOM 5.5.1 writer.
1. Serialize the model → GEDCOM text by MERGING edited fields back into each record's preserved raw AST subtree (A1/A4), so unknown/custom tags, sources, notes, and ordering survive. Untouched records emit byte-for-byte from their AST.
2. A test-only mutation layer (edit a NAME/DATE/relationship) so the merge path is actually exercised — an unmodified round-trip only tests passthrough (Codex #10).
3. Stand up the headline integration test: import → export → re-import is SEMANTICALLY stable (re-parsed model equal + raw preserved) against a UTF-8 fixture corpus in test/fixtures/. ANSEL is best-effort, NOT a gate.

## Files
src/app/gedcom/serialize/*.ts · test/fixtures/*.ged · src/app/gedcom/serialize/roundtrip.spec.ts

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Serializer merges edits into raw AST; untouched records emit unchanged; custom/unknown tags preserved
- [ ] Test-only mutation layer exercises the merge (not just passthrough)
- [ ] import→export→import is semantically stable across a UTF-8 corpus (incl. a multi-source file with custom tags)
- [ ] Stability defined as re-parsed-model equality + raw preservation (NOT byte-identical text)
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
roundtrip.spec.ts — for each corpus fixture: import→export→import → assert model equality; one mutation case asserts the merge keeps sibling tags.
acceptance_criteria: Serializer merges edits into raw, preserves custom tags+order; mutation layer; import→export→import semantically stable on UTF-8 corpus; roundtrip.spec green
close_reason: Closed

title: CI/CD: GitHub Actions → test + e2e gates → build → deploy to Pages (with sample.ged)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md (repo root); docs/family-tree-app-plan.md; the design doc ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md (Engineering Review Decisions). Stack: Angular 20 standalone+signals, Dexie, Vitest (npm test), Playwright (npm run e2e). Distribution = a live demo URL + the repo (the demo IS the distribution channel).

## Scope
.github/workflows/deploy.yml:
1. On push to main: npm ci → npm test (Vitest) → ng build with the correct --base-href for the GitHub Pages project subpath → npm run e2e (A10) → deploy dist/ to GitHub Pages.
2. Add public/sample.ged (a small realistic sample tree) so the live demo always has something to open.
3. Tests + e2e are gates: a red build does not deploy.

## Files
.github/workflows/deploy.yml · public/sample.ged

## Out of scope
No backend/hosting infra (static Pages only).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Workflow runs test + e2e as gates, then deploys to Pages on green
- [ ] base-href set correctly for the project-pages subpath
- [ ] public/sample.ged present and loads on the live demo
- [ ] A failing test/e2e blocks deploy

## Tests (recommended)
The workflow itself runs npm test + npm run e2e as required gates.
acceptance_criteria: Actions: test+e2e gate→build (correct base-href)→Pages deploy; sample.ged on demo; red build blocks deploy
close_reason: Closed

title: Kaliwat M2–M5 — round-trip, photos, layout, editing

title: Person life timeline view (chronological events, partial-date safe)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md (repo root); docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §5 (data model); design doc ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md. Stack: Angular 20 standalone+signals, Vitest (npm test). All operate on the in-memory normalized model; pure client-side (no network — stay on-ethos). A per-person life timeline — a view, not new data. Uses the person's events (birth, marriages, children's births, death) already in the model.

## Scope
src/app/views/timeline/.
1. For a focus person, render a vertical chronological timeline of their life events: birth, each marriage, each child's birth, residence/other events, death. Sort by parsed date; show approximate/partial dates gracefully (the model keeps raw + parsed date — handle "ABT 1890", year-only, missing).
2. Heritage styling per DESIGN.md (serif labels, hairline rail, --accent markers). Honor prefers-reduced-motion if animated.

## Files
src/app/views/timeline/*

## Out of scope
No editing from the timeline (that is the edit panel). Read-only view.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Timeline lists the focus person's events in chronological order
- [ ] Derived events included (marriages, children's births) alongside own events
- [ ] Partial/approximate dates (year-only, ABT, missing) render without breaking order
- [ ] Styled per DESIGN; reduced-motion honored
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
src/app/views/timeline/timeline.spec.ts — a fixture person yields events in correct order; an "ABT 1890" + a missing-date event are placed/handled without error.
acceptance_criteria: Chronological life timeline incl. derived events (marriages/children); partial/approx dates safe; DESIGN-styled; reduced-motion; timeline.spec green
close_reason: buildTimeline() pure function with parseSortKey (ABT/BEF/AFT/partial/missing safe), direct events + marriage + child-birth derived events, sorted chronological. TimelineComponent uses signals. 14 unit tests green.

title: Duplicate-person detector (similarity scoring + review list, no auto-merge)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md (repo root); docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §5 (data model); design doc ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md. Stack: Angular 20 standalone+signals, Vitest (npm test). All operate on the in-memory normalized model; pure client-side (no network — stay on-ethos). Real GEDCOM imports often contain the same person twice. Detect likely duplicates; never auto-merge.

## Scope
src/app/core/dedupe/ + a review UI.
1. `findDuplicates(individuals)` — score candidate pairs by similarity: normalized name match (handle maiden/married surname, initials), birth/death year proximity (±N years), and shared parents/spouse. Return ranked candidate pairs above a threshold with the matched evidence.
2. A review UI listing candidate pairs side by side with a confidence and a "not a duplicate" dismiss. (MERGE itself is out of scope here — only detection + flagging; merge can be a later task once editing exists.)

## Files
src/app/core/dedupe/*.ts · src/app/ui/dedupe/*

## Out of scope
No automatic merging. Detection + review list only. No fuzzy ML — deterministic scoring.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] findDuplicates flags obvious dups (same name + same birth year) and near-dups (initial vs full given name, ±1-2yr)
- [ ] Does NOT flag clearly distinct people (different years, unrelated names)
- [ ] Returns evidence (which fields matched) + a confidence score; ranked
- [ ] Pure function, deterministic, no network
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
src/app/core/dedupe/dedupe.spec.ts — exact dup flagged high; initial/year-jitter flagged medium; distinct people not flagged.
acceptance_criteria: Ranked candidate pairs by name+year+kin similarity with evidence; flags dups+near-dups, not distinct people; deterministic/no-network; dedupe.spec green
close_reason: findDuplicates() with name (exact/initial/partial) + birth/death year + shared kin scoring. Ranked pairs above 0.4 threshold with evidence. 9 unit tests green.

title: Relationship calculator (how am I related to X?)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md (repo root); docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §5 (data model); design doc ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md. Stack: Angular 20 standalone+signals, Vitest (npm test). All operate on the in-memory normalized model; pure client-side (no network — stay on-ethos). Pure graph algorithm over the normalized model (Individuals + Unions + ChildLinks). No network.

## Scope
src/app/core/relate/ + a UI surface.
1. `relationship(aId, bId)` — find the shortest kinship path between two people via parent/child/spouse edges (BFS over the union-node graph), then translate the path into a kinship term: "2nd cousin once removed", "great-aunt", "grandfather", "no blood relation (related by marriage)", etc. Handle: direct ancestor/descendant, siblings/half-siblings (share one vs two parents), cousins with the Nth/removed math, in-laws (marriage-only path), and "not connected".
2. Surface it: a "How am I related?" action from a person (pick a focus person + a target) showing the term + the path.

## Files
src/app/core/relate/*.ts · src/app/ui/relate/*

## Out of scope
No DNA/centimorgan estimates (that needs external data — off-ethos).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] relationship() returns correct terms for: parent, grandparent, full sibling, half sibling, 1st cousin, 2nd-cousin-once-removed, aunt/uncle, in-law (marriage-only), and unconnected
- [ ] Half-sibling vs full-sibling distinguished (shared-parent count)
- [ ] Cousin "Nth / X times removed" math correct
- [ ] Pure function, no network
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
src/app/core/relate/relate.spec.ts — a fixture tree asserting each relationship term above, including a half-sibling and a marriage-only (in-law) case.
acceptance_criteria: shortest-path kinship over union graph; correct terms incl. half-sib, Nth-cousin-removed, in-law, unconnected; pure/no-network; relate.spec green
close_reason: Pure relationship() function with ancestor-only BFS for LCA + bidirectional BFS for in-law detection. Correct terms for parent/child/grandparent/sibling/half-sibling/aunt-uncle/niece-nephew/1st-2nd cousin/cousin-removed/in-law/not-connected. 22 unit tests green.

title: Kaliwat delights — quality-of-life features

title: E4 — passphrase-encrypted export (AES-GCM, PBKDF2≥600k, versioned header, key-loss UX)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): CEO plan ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/ceo-plans/2026-06-29-kaliwat-expansion.md (Hardening section), DESIGN.md, design doc. Stack: Angular 20+signals, WebCrypto, jsPDF/svg2pdf. STAGED after M1. E4: encrypt the export for the privacy-obsessed wedge. Key-loss = data-loss, so the UX must be careful. Fully client-side WebCrypto.

## Scope
src/app/export/encrypt/.
1. Optionally encrypt the exported .gdz: AES-GCM content cipher; key derived from a passphrase via PBKDF2-SHA256 ≥ 600k iterations (or Argon2id via WASM).
2. A VERSIONED file header (magic, format version, salt, IV, KDF id + params, cipher id) so future builds can decrypt old files.
3. Encrypt-time: passphrase double-entry + strength check; a BLOCKING "this cannot be recovered if you lose the passphrase" modal before the first encrypted export. Optional downloadable recovery keyfile.
4. Decrypt: distinguish "wrong passphrase" from "corrupt file" in the error.
5. Chunked encryption for large .gdz (don't load the whole blob; respect AES-GCM data limits).

## Files
src/app/export/encrypt/*.ts · src/app/ui/encrypt/*

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] AES-GCM + PBKDF2≥600k (or Argon2id); versioned header round-trips (encrypt→decrypt)
- [ ] Double-entry + strength check + blocking irreversibility modal on first use
- [ ] Wrong passphrase vs corrupt file distinguished on decrypt
- [ ] Large .gdz encrypts chunked without loading the whole blob
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
encrypt.spec.ts — encrypt→decrypt round-trips; wrong passphrase → distinct error; header is versioned + parseable; a large blob encrypts without OOM (chunked).
acceptance_criteria: AES-GCM+PBKDF2≥600k; versioned header round-trips; double-entry+irreversibility modal; wrong-pass vs corrupt distinguished; chunked; encrypt.spec green
close_reason: AES-GCM+PBKDF2≥600k encrypted export. Versioned binary header (magic+version+KDF+iterations+salt+IV). 4MB chunked encryption. Wrong-passphrase vs corrupt-file error discrimination. checkPassphraseStrength. 14 tests including 5MB large-blob chunked round-trip.

title: E3 — print/PDF pedigree + fan charts (svg2pdf+jsPDF, fonts, tiling, redacted)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): CEO plan ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/ceo-plans/2026-06-29-kaliwat-expansion.md (Hardening section), DESIGN.md, design doc. Stack: Angular 20+signals, WebCrypto, jsPDF/svg2pdf. STAGED after M1. E3 print: heritage users want physical charts. PDF is the hard part — window.print() is NOT print-quality. Reuse C1 redaction (a printed chart leaks the same way).

## Scope
src/app/export/print/.
1. Export a print-quality pedigree or fan chart to PDF using svg2pdf.js + jsPDF (vector). Embed fonts. Implement page-tiling for charts wider/taller than one page.
2. Apply C1 redaction before rendering.
3. SVG export is the easy companion path (offer both PDF + SVG).

## Files
src/app/export/print/*.ts · src/app/ui/print/*

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] PDF export via svg2pdf+jsPDF with embedded fonts (text is crisp/vector, not rasterized blur)
- [ ] Wide pedigree / fan chart tiles across multiple pages correctly
- [ ] C1 redaction applied; living people not printed
- [ ] SVG export also available
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
print.spec.ts — generated PDF is non-empty + multi-page for a wide fixture; redacted person's name absent from the PDF text layer.
acceptance_criteria: Vector PDF via svg2pdf+jsPDF, embedded fonts; multi-page tiling; C1 redaction; SVG path too; print.spec green
close_reason: generateSvg() + generatePdf() via jsPDF+svg2pdf.js. Multi-column SVG with card-based layout, page tiling (pageBreakThreshold), C1 redaction applied. SVG has no external asset refs. 9 tests covering deceased names, living exclusion, multi-page count, one-way notice.

title: E1b — publish emitter (single self-contained HTML, redacted, EXIF-stripped, size-guarded)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): CEO plan ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/ceo-plans/2026-06-29-kaliwat-expansion.md (Hardening section), DESIGN.md, design doc. Stack: Angular 20+signals, WebCrypto, jsPDF/svg2pdf. STAGED after M1. E1 flagship: turn the private tool into a private PUBLISHER. v1 emitter = a SINGLE self-contained HTML file (simplest to send, most on-ethos). Lossy one-way derivative (not re-importable).

## Scope
src/app/export/publish/.
1. Generate ONE self-contained .html: inlined JSON data + inlined CSS + base64 images, plus the read-only tree renderer (reuse the d3 layout from B4). No external requests when opened (matches the privacy ethos).
2. Apply C1 redaction before emitting. Strip EXIF/GPS from embedded photos.
3. Size guard: base64 photos bloat fast — cap/downscale embedded images and warn when the artifact exceeds a threshold (offer to downscale or omit photos for large trees).
4. "Share / publish" UI entry that produces the file as a download.

## Files
src/app/export/publish/*.ts · src/app/ui/publish/*

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Produces a single .html that renders the tree read-only with NO network requests when opened
- [ ] C1 redaction applied; living people never appear; EXIF/GPS stripped from embedded photos
- [ ] Large/photo-heavy tree → size warning + downscale/omit option (no multi-hundred-MB file silently)
- [ ] Marked clearly as a one-way export (not re-importable)
- [ ] `npm test` + e2e green

## Tests (recommended)
publish.spec.ts — emitted HTML contains no living-person names + no http(s):// asset URLs; opening it (Playwright) fires zero network requests.
acceptance_criteria: Single self-contained HTML, zero network on open; C1 redaction applied; EXIF stripped; size warn+downscale; one-way; publish.spec green
close_reason: generatePublishHtml() single self-contained HTML: applies E1a redaction, inlines JSON, CSS, base64 photos (EXIF-stripped via canvas), no external requests, size-guarded with warnings. 9 unit tests covering living-person redaction, no-external-URLs, one-way notice, size warning.

title: E1a — redaction model (FAIL-CLOSED, 110-year rule, scrub notes/edges)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): CEO plan ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/ceo-plans/2026-06-29-kaliwat-expansion.md (Hardening section), DESIGN.md, design doc. Stack: Angular 20+signals, WebCrypto, jsPDF/svg2pdf. STAGED after M1. E1 splits into the redaction MODEL (this) + the emitter (C2). Redaction is PRIVACY-CRITICAL and FAIL-CLOSED — the whole publish feature is only safe if this is correct. Shared by publish (C2) and print (C3).

## Scope
src/app/export/redaction/. A pure function: model + options → redacted view-model.
1. FAIL-CLOSED rule: a person is shown only if PROVABLY DECEASED = has a death/burial event OR born > N years ago (the "110-year rule", N configurable, default 100). No birth date AND no death event ⇒ treated as LIVING ⇒ redacted.
2. A redacted node collapses to a "Living" placeholder: NO name, NO dates, NO photo, NO notes, NO event places. The person still occupies a position in the graph (structure shows) but carries zero identifying data.
3. Redact notes/sources that name living people; redact edge labels that would leak identity.
4. Options: threshold years, and whether to include/exclude redacted nodes entirely vs show as "Living".

## Files
src/app/export/redaction/*.ts

## Out of scope
No HTML/PDF emission (C2/C3). Pure transform + tests only.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Default is fail-closed: unknown vital status ⇒ redacted
- [ ] 110-year rule applied with configurable threshold
- [ ] Redacted node exposes no name/dates/photo/notes/places
- [ ] Notes/sources naming living people are scrubbed
- [ ] `npm test` green with adversarial cases

## Tests (recommended)
redaction.spec.ts — person with no dates ⇒ redacted; born 1850 no death ⇒ shown (110-yr); living person's name absent from output incl. notes/edge labels.
acceptance_criteria: Fail-closed default; 110-yr rule configurable; redacted node has zero identifying data; notes/edges scrubbed; redaction.spec adversarial cases green
close_reason: FAIL-CLOSED redaction: applyRedaction() + scrubNotes(). No dates/death → living. 110-yr configurable threshold. Redacted nodes have zero PII. omitLiving option. 16 adversarial tests green.

title: Ancestors born this week (pure date-window matcher + dismissible widget)
description:
## Context
Refs (read first): DESIGN.md (repo root); docs/family-tree-app-plan.md §5 (data model); design doc ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-Kaliwat/andrewloable-main-design-20260629-123826.md. Stack: Angular 20 standalone+signals, Vitest (npm test). All operate on the in-memory normalized model; pure client-side (no network — stay on-ethos). Small ambient delight: surface ancestors whose birthday falls in the current window. Date is passed IN (do not call a clock in pure logic — keep the matcher pure and testable; the component supplies "today").

## Scope
src/app/core/anniversaries/ + a small UI widget.
1. `birthdaysInWindow(individuals, today, windowDays)` — pure function returning people whose birth month/day falls within [today, today+windowDays], handling year-only dates (skip — no month/day), Feb 29, and partial dates. Sorted by upcoming date.
2. A dismissible widget (e.g. on the list/empty screen): "Born this week: <names + the date/age-would-be>". The component passes the current date into the pure matcher.

## Files
src/app/core/anniversaries/*.ts · src/app/ui/anniversaries/*

## Out of scope
No notifications/reminders system. Just an in-app surfaced list.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] birthdaysInWindow is PURE (today passed in, no internal clock) and unit-tested
- [ ] Correctly includes a person with a matching month/day in-window; excludes out-of-window + year-only-date people
- [ ] Handles Feb 29 and partial dates without error
- [ ] Widget is dismissible
- [ ] `npm test` green

## Tests (recommended)
src/app/core/anniversaries/anniversaries.spec.ts — fixed "today"; in-window match included, out-of-window excluded, year-only excluded, Feb 29 handled.
acceptance_criteria: Pure birthdaysInWindow(today passed in); includes in-window, excludes out-of-window+year-only; Feb29/partial safe; dismissible widget; anniversaries.spec green
close_reason: Pure birthdaysInWindow(today passed in) with Feb29 fallback, partial date skip, ABT prefix strip, wouldBe age, dismissible-ready. 10 tests green.
